steps.message: Apply the `X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN` or `DENY` header to the `/payments/checkout` path to prevent framing, directly addressing the missing header identified by the security audit.
Verify the successful application of the `X-Frame-Options` header to the `/payments/checkout` path by performing an automated scan or querying WAF logs for the specified URL.
Initiate an investigation into the root cause of the missing `X-Frame-Options` header for `/payments/checkout` to understand why the security control was absent during deployment or configuration.

Seek approval for the long-term Clickjacking prevention strategy, which could involve a global WAF rule for `X-Frame-Options` or implementing a comprehensive Content Security Policy (CSP) with `frame-ancestors` across all critical web assets.

Deploy the approved long-term Clickjacking mitigation strategy, focusing on either a WAF rule to enforce `X-Frame-Options` globally or updating application code to include a `Content-Security-Policy` with `frame-ancestors` directive.
Incident PB-CLICKJACK-001 for Clickjacking vulnerability on `/payments/checkout` has been contained and long-term remediation initiated. Notifying relevant teams and closing the incident ticket.

params.text: Incident PB-CLICKJACK-001 (Clickjacking on /payments/checkout) resolved. X-Frame-Options header applied, long-term strategy in progress. Incident ticket closed.
